content:
First write \(r=[r]+ \{r\}=n+f\) with \(n=[r]\in\{0,1,2,\dots\}\) and \(f=\{r\}\in[0,1)\).  The equation is

\[
25f+n=125.
\]

Since \(0\le25f<25\), we must have
\[
n\le125< n+25
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
0\le125-n<25
\quad\Longrightarrow\quad
n\in\{101,102,\dots,125\}.
\]
For each such \(n\),
\[
f=\frac{125-n}{25},
\]
and
\[
r=n+f
= n+\frac{125-n}{25}
=\frac{24n+125}{25}.
\]
We sum over \(n=101\) to \(125\).  There are \(25\) terms and
\(\sum_{n=101}^{125}n=\frac{(101+125)\cdot25}{2}=2825\), so
\[
\sum r
=\sum_{n=101}^{125}\frac{24n+125}{25}
=\frac{1}{25}\Bigl(24\sum_{n=101}^{125}n+125\cdot25\Bigr)
=\frac{1}{25}(24\cdot2825+3125)
=\frac{70925}{25}
=2837.
\]
Hence the desired sum is \(\boxed{2837}.\)
